summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened with energetic greetings and a lighthearted discussion about navigation apps, using them as a metaphor for spiritual guidance. He contrasted the enemy's destructive path with the life-giving way of Jesus, framing the message around the single verse John 10:10. Pastor Scott taught that the enemy, like a bad GPS, seeks to lead us to unwanted destinations through a three-part strategy: to steal what belongs to us, to kill our faith, and to destroy our lives. He illustrated life using an hourglass, representing the past, the present, and future potential, emphasizing that our choices in the 'present' moment are critical. Williams shared vulnerably from his own experience with divorce to highlight that even when we do everything right, the enemy attacks, but we must not become an 'accessory' to his crimes by allowing his whispers to influence us. He warned that the enemy aims to chip away at our beliefs, create division, and blur moral lines, especially in areas like relationships, identity, and politics. The sermon culminated in the powerful contrast of the verse: while the thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy, Jesus comes to give abundant life. Pastor Scott called the congregation to reject being an accessory to the enemy's work, to hold fast to God's truth about their identity, and to surrender fully to Christ for the life only He can provide, leading to a prayer of commitment and salvation.
mainPoints: The enemy's sole purpose is to steal what belongs to you, kill your faith, and destroy your life, and we must refuse to be an accessory to his crimes.
The enemy seeks to sacrifice what you believe for what he believes, using division and confusion to chip away at your faith and identity in Christ.
In contrast to the enemy's destructive mission, Jesus came to give abundant life, offering restoration and hope for every broken area when we surrender to Him.

versesMentioned.text: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.

Monday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel the enemy is trying to 'steal' your peace, joy, or a good thing in your life. Write it down.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to the enemy's schemes. Help me to recognize what he is trying to steal from me today and to hold fast to what You have given me.

Tuesday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a thought or habit where you have cooperated with temptation. Make a conscious decision to not be an 'accessory' to it today.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I refuse to partner with the enemy's plans. Forgive me for the times I have been an accessory. Give me strength to walk in obedience today.

Wednesday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Wednesday.action: Read one promise from God's Word (e.g., Jeremiah 29:11, Romans 8:37) and meditate on it, countering any lie that challenges your belief.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, my faith is in You. When doubts or worldly voices arise, help me to remember and stand on Your unchanging truth.

Thursday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Thursday.action: In a conversation or on social media today, intentionally choose unity over division. Listen respectfully to someone with a different viewpoint.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, where there is potential for division, let me be a peacemaker. Help me to disagree agreeably and to reflect Your love.

Friday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Friday.focus: Embracing the abundant life Jesus offers.
Friday.action: Do one thing today that brings you genuine, God-honoring life and joy—spend time in nature, connect with a positive friend, serve someone, or worship.
Friday.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for coming to give me abundant life. I receive Your life and joy today. Fill me up so I can pour out to others.

Saturday.scripture: John 10:10 - 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.'
Saturday.action: Take a quiet moment to hand over one situation that feels 'destroyed' or broken to God. Symbolically write it on a piece of paper and pray over it.
Saturday.prayer: God, I bring my broken pieces to You. I believe You can bring life to what feels dead. I trust You with my past, present, and future. Make me new.
